title: Decaisnina pedicellata
intro: Decaisnina pedicellata, also known as the Australian fish poison tree or fish poison plant, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of D. pedicellata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea form, although it is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxic properties.
taxonomy: Decaisnina pedicellata is a species of plant in the family Erythroxylaceae. It is a flowering plant native to Australia and is also known as the quandong or native peach. The classification of Decaisnina pedicellata includes being a member of the genus Decaisnina and the family Erythroxylaceae within the order Malpighiales. Decaisnina pedicellata is an important part of Australian flora and is used in various traditional and modern applications.
